title: Math — Circles
description: SAT-style circle questions with clean MC structure and deeply explained, step-by-step solutions.

questions.prompt: A circle has center (−1, 1). A line is tangent to the circle at the point (5, −4). Which of the following points lies on the tangent line?

solution.approach: Use the perpendicular relationship between a radius and a tangent line, then test which point satisfies the tangent line’s slope.
steps: The radius connects the center of the circle to the point of tangency. Its endpoints are (−1, 1) and (5, −4).
Compute the slope of the radius: (−4 − 1) / (5 − (−1)) = −5 / 6.
A tangent line is always perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency, so its slope is the negative reciprocal of −5/6, which is 6/5.
Now test each answer choice by computing the slope between (5, −4) and the candidate point.
For (10, 2): slope = (2 − (−4)) / (10 − 5) = 6 / 5, which matches the tangent slope.
solution.takeaway: At the point of tangency, the radius and tangent form a 90° angle, so their slopes are negative reciprocals.

questions.prompt: The length of arc ADC is 5π and its central angle is 100°. What is the length of the remaining arc of the circle?

solution.approach: Use the proportional relationship between arc length and central angle.
steps: Arc length is directly proportional to the measure of its central angle.
The full circle measures 360°, so the remaining arc corresponds to 360° − 100° = 260°.
Set up a proportion: (remaining arc length) / (5π) = 260 / 100.
Solve: remaining arc = 5π × (260 / 100) = 13π.
solution.takeaway: When radius is unknown, compare arcs using angle ratios instead of formulas.

questions.prompt: The equation x² + x + y² + y = 199/2 represents a circle. What is the radius of the circle?

solution.approach: Rewrite the equation in standard form by completing the square.
steps: Group x-terms and y-terms: (x² + x) + (y² + y) = 199/2.
Complete the square for x: x² + x + 1/4 = (x + 1/2)².
Complete the square for y: y² + y + 1/4 = (y + 1/2)².
Since 1/4 was added twice, add 1/2 to the right side: 199/2 + 1/2 = 200/2 = 100.
The equation becomes (x + 1/2)² + (y + 1/2)² = 100, so r² = 100 and r = 10.
solution.takeaway: After completing the square, the constant on the right side equals r².

questions.prompt: The equation (x + 4)² + (y − 19)² = 121 represents a circle. Which value could be the x-coordinate of a point on the circle?

solution.approach: Determine the horizontal range of the circle using center ± radius.
steps: From the equation, the center is (−4, 19) and the radius is √121 = 11.
The x-values on the circle range from −4 − 11 = −15 to −4 + 11 = 7.
Check answer choices: only −14 falls within this interval.
solution.takeaway: A coordinate on a circle must lie within one radius horizontally and vertically from the center.

questions.prompt: What is the center of the circle defined by x² + 20x + y² + 16y = −20?

solution.approach: Complete the square to identify the center directly.
steps: Group terms: (x² + 20x) + (y² + 16y) = −20.
Complete the square: x² + 20x + 100 = (x + 10)².
Complete the square: y² + 16y + 64 = (y + 8)².
Add 100 and 64 to both sides: −20 + 164 = 144.
The equation becomes (x + 10)² + (y + 8)² = 144, so the center is (−10, −8).
solution.takeaway: The signs inside squared expressions are opposite the center’s coordinates.

questions.prompt: A circle has radius 18. If the central angle of an arc is 120°, what is the arc length?

solution.approach: Convert degrees to radians, then apply the arc length formula.
steps: Arc length formula is s = rθ, where θ must be in radians.
Convert 120° to radians: 120° × (π / 180°) = 2π / 3.
Substitute: s = 18 × (2π / 3) = 12π.
solution.takeaway: The arc length formula only works with radians.

questions.prompt: A circle has diameter endpoints (2,4) and (2,14). What is the radius of the circle?

solution.approach: Find the diameter length, then divide by 2.
steps: Both points share the same x-coordinate, so the diameter is vertical.
Distance between endpoints is |14 − 4| = 10.
Radius is half the diameter: 10 ÷ 2 = 5.
solution.takeaway: When points align vertically or horizontally, distance simplifies to subtraction.

questions.prompt: Circle A has equation (x + 5)² + (y − 5)² = 4. Circle B has the same center and twice the radius. What is the value of k in Circle B’s equation?

solution.approach: Track how changing radius affects r² in the equation.
steps: From Circle A, r² = 4 so r = 2.
Doubling the radius gives r = 4.
Square the new radius: r² = 16.
solution.takeaway: Doubling a radius multiplies r² by four.

questions.prompt: What is the diameter of the circle (x − 5)² + (y − 3)² = 16?

solution.approach: Extract the radius from the equation, then double it.
steps: The equation shows r² = 16.
Take the square root: r = 4.
Diameter = 2r = 8.
solution.takeaway: Circle equations always store the square of the radius.

questions.prompt: The measure of arc RS is 100°. What is the measure of its central angle?

solution.approach: Apply the defining property of central angles.
steps: A central angle has its vertex at the center of the circle.
A central angle’s measure is always equal to the measure of its intercepted arc.
solution.takeaway: Central angles match their arcs exactly.
